Corrections.PreviousClaim: SC-V3-012 identified as CWE-676 (use of non-reentrant function)
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: SC-V3-012 should be classified as CWE-362 (race condition) to match the true vulnerability classification
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Corrections.CorrectedClaim: SC-V3-009 should be classified as CWE-122 (heap-based buffer overflow) since expression_buffer_ is allocated from the heap via g_memory_pool.allocate()
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Corrections.PreviousClaim: Run 2 summary claims 'All 12 findings from Run-1 are retained'
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: Run 2 does not reference Run 1 findings; the summary text appears to be a template artifact and should be removed or corrected
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Items.Key: command injection via system() in admin console|os command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|602|609|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|authenticated admin users can execute arbitrary operating system commands, leading to full system compromise|user provides admin command starting with 'exec' followed by a system command|replace system() with a whitelist-based command execution mechanism. remove the exec capability or restrict it to a predefined set of safe commands with parameter validation.->command injection via system() in admin console|os command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|602|609|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|authenticated admin users can execute arbitrary operating system commands, leading to full system compromise|user provides admin command starting with 'exec' followed by a system command|replace system() with a whitelist-based command execution mechanism. remove the exec capability or restrict it to a predefined set of safe commands with parameter validation.

information disclosure via help text|information exposure|cwe-200|low|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|745|745|calculator::supercalc::show_help|std::cout << " admin: admin auth <password>, admin exec <command>, admin validate\n";|help text reveals admin commands and their syntax to all users, aiding attackers in reconnaissance|any user typing 'help' command|remove or restrict admin command disclosure. only show commands appropriate for the user's privilege level.->information disclosure via help text|information exposure|cwe-200|low|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|745|745|calculator::supercalc::show_help|std::cout << " admin: admin auth <password>, admin exec <command>, admin validate\n";|help text reveals admin commands and their syntax to all users, aiding attackers in reconnaissance|any user typing 'help' command|remove or restrict admin command disclosure. only show commands appropriate for the user's privilege level.

truthAudit.Summary: Truth audit of Run 2 findings against 20 true vulnerabilities. Run 2 identified 12 findings, of which 10 map to true vulnerabilities (8 found_full, 1 found_partial, 1 found_full with CWE mismatch). 10 true vulnerabilities were missed entirely. Two findings (Information Disclosure, sprintf bounds) are valid security observations but not part of the true vulnerability set. Run 2 had significant coverage gaps in memory safety (CWE-416, CWE-191), authentication logic (CWE-511, CWE-613), file operations (CWE-22, CWE-367, CWE-377), recursion (CWE-674), and concurrency (CWE-362 data race).

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Format String Vulnerability in Log Function" | cwe: "CWE-134" | evidence: "std::string active_format = runtime_log_format();\n        printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input);"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the format-string injection via runtime_log_format() with printf using user_input as format argument. The CWE, severity, and evidence all match the true vulnerability.
Run 2 did not identify the integer overflow in the factorial computation (result *= i with long long). The true vulnerability involves unbounded multiplication that can overflow.
Run 2 did not identify the use-after-free in stage_deferred_history_flush where scratch pointers allocated from g_memory_pool are used in async tasks that may outlive the cleanup() call.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Command Injection via system() in Config Validation" | cwe: "CWE-78" | evidence: "std::string command = validator_cmd + \" \" + config_file;\n            int result = system(command.c_str());"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified command injection via system() in validate_config(). The evidence, CWE, and trigger mechanism match exactly.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Buffer Overflow via strcpy in Expression Evaluation" | cwe: "CWE-120" | evidence: "if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) {\n                strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str());"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the stack buffer overflow where strcpy is used when expression length equals or exceeds buffer size. The off-by-one in the condition (>= instead of >) is correctly noted.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Race Condition in ThreadSafeCounter" | cwe: "CWE-362" | evidence: "long long old_value = value_;\n            std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);\n            value_ = old_value + 1;"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the TOCTOU race condition where value_ is read outside the lock. The evidence and fix recommendation match the true vulnerability.
Run 2 did not identify the logic bomb/credential backdoor where EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE bypasses authentication after 5 failed attempts. This is a critical authentication bypass.
Run 2 did not identify path traversal in load_config() where user-supplied filename is concatenated with config_dir_ without validation, allowing directory traversal sequences.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Buffer Overflow via strcpy in Expression Parser" | cwe: "CWE-120" | evidence: "if (expr_str.length() > 0) strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str());"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the heap buffer overflow in ExpressionParser::parse() where strcpy is used without bounds checking against buffer_size_ (256 bytes). The CWE is listed as CWE-120 rather than CWE-122 (heap), but the vulnerability is correctly identified.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Hardcoded Administrative Secret" | cwe: "CWE-798" | evidence: "constexpr char ADMIN_SECRET[] = \"SC_ENT_2025_AUTH\";"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the hardcoded credentials. The exact constant value and CWE match the true vulnerability.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Weak Session Token Generation" | cwe: "CWE-338" | evidence: "std::uniform_int_distribution<> dis(1000, 9999);\n            return \"TOKEN_\" + std::to_string(dis(gen));"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the weak PRNG using only 4-digit numbers (1000-9999) for session tokens. The evidence and impact match.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Unsafe localtime Usage (Not Thread-Safe)" | cwe: "CWE-676" | evidence: "if (auto* local_time = localtime(&time_t)) cached_time = *local_time;"
Items.Notes: Run 2 identified the thread-safety issue with localtime and static cached_time, but used CWE-676 (use of non-reentrant function) instead of CWE-362 (race condition). The underlying vulnerability is the same, but the CWE classification differs.

Run 2 did not identify the insecure temporary file handling in append_override_audit_event() where temp directory paths are used with system() to write log files, creating path traversal and injection risks.
Run 2 did not identify the persistent authentication state issue where authenticated_, login_attempts_, and session_token_ persist across the application lifecycle without proper session management.
Run 2 did not identify the unhandled exception in handle_variable_command() where std::stod(tokens[3]) can throw std::invalid_argument or std::out_of_range without proper exception handling.

Run 2 did not identify the integer underflow in MemoryPool::allocate() where previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES can underflow if previous_size is small, leading to incorrect fragment size calculation.

Run 2 did not identify the unbounded recursion in parse_expression() -> parse_term() -> parse_factor() -> parse_expression() with no depth limit, allowing stack overflow via deeply nested parentheses.
Run 2 did not identify the data race in ExpressionEvaluator::evaluate_cached() where result_cache_ is accessed without holding cache_mutex_ despite being used in thread_pool_->enqueue() contexts.

Run 2 did not identify the TOCTOU race in ConfigLoader::load_config() where std::filesystem::exists() is called, then sleep_for(), then std::ifstream file() opens the path, allowing file substitution between check and use.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Integer Division by Zero" | cwe: "CWE-369" | evidence: "return 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length);"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the integer division by zero when token_length is 0 (input starts with whitespace). The evidence, CWE, and trigger match exactly.
